title: Hidden internal config-merge CLI hook
action: Add a pure `pub fn run_internal_merge_config(path: &std::path::Path) -> i32` to src/config/merge.rs that calls merge_config_on_upgrade(path): on Ok(backup_path), print the backup path to stdout (a caller capturing this process's stdout uses it for a later rollback, per the EP5 design correction in approach.md) and return 0; on Err(e), print the error to stderr and return 1 -- never panic. Wire a new HIDDEN CLI verb into src/main.rs's manual arg parser (`parse_args`, around line 60-96): add an `internal_merge_config: Option<String>` field to `Args`, match `"--internal-merge-config" => internal_merge_config = it.next()` in the match arm (mirroring `--config`'s `it.next()` pattern), and in `main()` add an early-exit branch BEFORE `migrate_default_state_if` is called (main.rs:151) -- i.e. as the very first branch in main(), before any legacy-state migration -- mirroring the existing `doctor`/`service` early-exit pattern (main.rs:153-163) but placed earlier so this internal, self-exec-only path never triggers legacy-state migration as a side effect: `if let Some(path) = &args.internal_merge_config { std::process::exit(herdr_go::config::merge::run_internal_merge_config(std::path::Path::new(path))); }`. This flag is an internal implementation detail for the self-update feature (EP5's self-exec step) -- it must NEVER appear in `print_help()`'s output and is not a documented public command. Do not change any other existing argument, help text, or CLI behavior.

truths: run_internal_merge_config returns 0 and prints the backup path to stdout when merge_config_on_upgrade succeeds
run_internal_merge_config returns a nonzero code and prints an error to stderr when merge_config_on_upgrade fails, without panicking
print_help()'s output does not mention --internal-merge-config anywhere
Every existing CLI argument (doctor, service <verb>, --config, --demo, --bind, --help) still parses and behaves exactly as before

artifacts.substantive: exports run_internal_merge_config with the 2 named unit tests, no TODO stubs
Args gains internal_merge_config field, parse_args handles the hidden flag, main() dispatches to run_internal_merge_config and exits -- print_help unchanged

key_links: main.rs's new branch calls config::merge::run_internal_merge_config, never reimplementing merge dispatch inline
prohibitions: --internal-merge-config must never appear in print_help()'s output
No change to any other existing argument's parsing or behavior

verify: grep -q 'fn run_internal_merge_config' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'internal_merge_config' src/main.rs && grep -q 'fn run_internal_merge_config_returns_zero_and_prints_backup_path_on_success' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn run_internal_merge_config_returns_nonzero_on_merge_failure' src/config/merge.rs && ! (cargo run --quiet -- --help 2>&1 | grep -qi 'internal-merge-config') && cargo test --quiet config

trace.outcome: Added pub fn run_internal_merge_config to src/config/merge.rs (wraps merge_config_on_upgrade, prints backup path/error, never panics) with 2 unit tests; wired a hidden --internal-merge-config CLI verb into main.rs's parse_args/Args and an early-exit branch in main() before migrate_default_state_if, so this self-exec-only path never triggers legacy-state migration. print_help() unchanged; flag never appears in --help output.
trace.verify_output: All 4 grep checks passed; --help output has no mention of internal-merge-config; cargo test --quiet config: 73 passed, 0 failed (includes the 2 new run_internal_merge_config tests). Exit code 0.
trace.verify_command: grep -q 'fn run_internal_merge_config' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'internal_merge_config' src/main.rs && grep -q 'fn run_internal_merge_config_returns_zero_and_prints_backup_path_on_success' src/config/merge.rs && grep -q 'fn run_internal_merge_config_returns_nonzero_on_merge_failure' src/config/merge.rs && ! (cargo run --quiet -- --help 2>&1 | grep -qi 'internal-merge-config') && cargo test --quiet config
